title: Valiya Koonambaikulam temple
tagline: Valiya Koonambaikulam temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Valiya Koonambaikulam temple or Valia Koonampayikulam temple is a Hindu temple located at Koonambaikulam near Vadakkevila in Kollam district of Kerala in India.
overview.description: Valiya Koonambaikulam temple or Valia Koonampayikulam temple is a Hindu temple located at Koonambaikulam near Vadakkevila in Kollam district of Kerala in India. This is one of the most ancient temples in Kerala and is dedicated to Bhadrakali (popularly known as 'Koonambaikulathamma', which means The mother of Koonambaikulam). The temple is under the control of Valiya Koonambaikulam Sree Bhadrakali Kshetra Trust. The trust owns an Engineering college called Valia Koonambaikulathamma College of Engineering and Technology (VKCET) at Parippally in Kollam district.

topPlaces.name: Vadakkevila
topPlaces.description: Vadakkevila is a village and neighbourhood of Kollam city, in Kollam district. This village is under the governance of Kollam corporation.

topPlaces.name: Kolloorvila Juma Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Kolloorvila Juma Mosque, also known as the Kolloorvila Juma Masjid, is a Friday mosque situated at Pallimukku, 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) from Kollam city centre, in the state of Kerala, India. The mosque is one of the largest mosques in Kerala and can accommodate more than 5,000 worshippers.

topPlaces.name: Thattamala
topPlaces.description: Thattamala is a neighbourhood situated at the southeast border of Kollam city in state of Kerala, India. It is situated at National Highways – NH 66 (Earlier NH 47).

topPlaces.name: Mevaram
topPlaces.description: Mevaram, is a neighbourhood and a junction in the city of Kollam in the Indian state of Kerala. The Kollam Bypass, which forms part of National Highway-66, begins from Mevaram junction.

topPlaces.name: Punthalathazham
topPlaces.description: Punthalathazham is a landlocked neighbourhood of the city of Kollam in the Indian state of Kerala, around six kilometres east of the city centre. Punthalathazham is in Kollam Taluk and comes under Vadakkevila Village having a population under 5000.

topPlaces.name: Eravipuram
topPlaces.description: Eravipuram is a neighbourhood of Kollam city in Kerala, India. It is one of the six zones of the Kollam City Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Polayathode
topPlaces.description: Polayathode or Polayathodu is one of the fastest growing neighbourhoods of the city of Kollam, in the state of Kerala, India. It is situated at National Highways – NH 66 (Earlier NH 47).

topPlaces.name: Kollam
topPlaces.description: Kollam (Malayalam: [kolːɐm] ;), is an ancient seaport and the fourth largest city in the Indian state of Kerala. Located on the southern tip of the Malabar Coast of the Arabian Sea, the city is on the banks of Ashtamudi Lake and is 71 kilometres (44 mi) northwest of the Thiruvananthapuram.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Valiya Koonambaikulam temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Valiya Koonambaikulam temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Valiya Koonambaikulam temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (54 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Valiya Koonambaikulam temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Valiya Koonambaikulam temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Valiya Koonambaikulam temple?
faq.a: Stays in Valiya Koonambaikulam temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Valiya Koonambaikulam temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vadakkevila, Kolloorvila Juma Mosque, Thattamala, Mevaram, Punthalathazham.
